headline: Meituan's open 1.6T-parameter LongCat-2.0 anchors a month dominated by large sparse models and a wave of new agent and retrieval techniques.
body: Meituan released LongCat-2.0, a 1.6 trillion parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with sparse attention activating roughly 48 billion parameters per token, trained on AI ASIC superpods rather than GPUs and uploaded to Hugging Face. A cluster of technique concepts broke out together this window, including agent skills, vulnerability detection, vector search, trajectory data, behavioral auditing, ablation, and masking. On the product side, Base44 launched its own model to seek defensibility, Open Memory Protocol proposed a unified memory store across Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor, and commentary tracked the closing gap between open-source and frontier models alongside rising adoption of cheaper Chinese models. Anthropic and Microsoft continued contesting agent integration into workplace platforms like Teams and Slack.
